title: arenaria modesta subsp. algarbiensis
intro: Arenaria modesta subsp. algarbiensis, also known as the Algarve sandwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae), native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Portugal, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Arenaria modesta subsp. algarbiensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
